Codex Review

  Target: working tree diff

  The component split preserves most page content, but the new Head component regresses the previously working SEO/social metadata by rendering placeholder strings instead of computed values.

  Review comment:

  - [P2] Restore evaluated metadata values in head — src/components/Head.astro:10-12
  Because these attributes now wrap Astro expressions in quotes and the file no longer defines siteDescription, siteUrl, previewImage, or timelineSchema, the rendered head emits literal values like
  {siteDescription}/{siteUrl} and a non-computed JSON-LD string. This breaks canonical links, descriptions, and social preview metadata for every page using this layout; these values need to be defined and passed
  as Astro expressions rather than quoted placeholders.
Give me a good conventional commit message to use for these changes. Do not include yourself as a coauthor
push the branch and create a new PR to push back to the source repo

@statusline-command.sh Please make the following changes:
Batch jq calls — A single jq invocation extracting all fields would cut ~20 process forks down to 1.
Remove dead code — format_commas, used_comma, remain_comma.
Tighten /tmp permissions — umask 077 or use a private temp directory.
Optionally remove the update checker — It phones home to GitHub and is only useful if you want auto-update notifications.
@statusline-command.sh If I used API pricing instead of a Claude subscription, how could I track how much money I am spending in the current session in my statusline?
I want to add that to my statusline now but leave it commented out. I plan to use it on my work computer that uses API pricing instead of a subscription
Can you also make it easy for me to know where I need to comment out for the 5hour and weekly reset information since the API does not have the same limitations
I need the cache file directory to be somewhere in $HOME
